seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: keep the min-length degree-capped connectivity goal but (a) restrict the output so every marker has a per-marker collar cap in {2,3,4} (degree-constrained, NP-hard), and (b) generalize the input by splitting markers into mandatory artifact chambers (terminals) and optional relay posts (Steiner junction candidates) -- only the chambers must be connected, so the polynomial Euclidean MST becomes a degree-constrained Steiner tree.
title: Buried Cable Trenches: Degree-Capped Artifact Comms on a Dig Grid
strategies: chamber chain baseline: link the artifact chambers in input order into a single path (always feasible, matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)
nearest-neighbour chamber path: start at a chamber and repeatedly hop to the closest unwired chamber, forming a short degree-2 path (ignores relay posts)

degree-constrained Kruskal over chambers: add globally shortest chamber-chamber trenches whose endpoints still have free collars and that merge two components, then repair leftover components via spare-collar leaves; keep the shorter of tree vs NN path
Steiner routing through relay posts: exploit the optional posts as junction points (Fermat/Steiner topology) and apply length-reducing edge-swap local search that respects the collar caps
